description_long: This is a small, orange-brown crustacean with a robust, slightly flattened body and two prominent, asymmetrical claws. The larger claw is white-tipped and highly exaggerated, typical of a fiddler crab, while the smaller claw is more slender and less conspicuous. The carapace is smooth and slightly glossy, with subtle segmentation visible at the joints of the legs and claws. The legs are thin, jointed, and covered with fine setae, radiating outward from the body and ending in pointed tips. The eyes are mounted on short stalks, positioned at the front corners of the carapace, giving the crab a wide field of view. The overall appearance is naturalistic, with realistic coloration and textural details that suggest a hard, chitinous exoskeleton.
description: A small, orange-brown crab with one oversized white-tipped claw, thin jointed legs, and stalked eyes on a smooth, glossy carapace.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing both claws, with the large white-tipped claw raised and the eyes clearly visible on stalks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the exaggerated size difference between the two claws and the slender, jointed legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, slightly arched carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the smaller claw and the curvature of the body and legs.
